sanskrit: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः। वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः॥
transliteration: yām imāṃ puṣpitāṃ vācaṃ pravadanty avipaścitaḥ | veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Which flowery speech the unwise proclaim, Veda-talk-delighting, O Pārtha, saying 'There is nothing else.'
contextual_meaning: Krishna exposes the psychological trap of using spiritual language to justify material craving—when ritualism becomes a socially sanctioned addiction to future rewards rather than present transformation.
psychological_state: Performative spirituality—using sacred words to plaster over the gnawing sense that ‘is this all there is?’
trigger_scenario: Buying a $2000 yoga-retreat package that promises ‘manifest abundance’ while secretly hoping it will finally make you feel enough.
anchor_text: Don’t let rituals become shopping lists for heavenly goodies.
